title: Sorting Array of Integers (Easy BUG FIX)

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a method `bubble_sort` that sorts the array in ascending order using Bubble Sort algorithm. The method should return a boolean indicating whether the array was sorted or not. Note: Do not use the built-in sort functions.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a list of integers separated by spaces, where the first line contains the count of integers, followed by the integers themselves.
statement.outputFormat: Return a boolean (true or false) indicating whether the array was sorted or not.
statement.notes: Bubble sort has a time complexity of O(n^2), where n is the number of elements in the array. This is a simple implementation and may not be suitable for large arrays.

examples.explanation: The input array [1, 2, 3] is already sorted in ascending order.
The input array [5, 2, 8, 1, 9] is not sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: Start from the beginning of the array and compare each pair of adjacent elements. If they are in the wrong order, swap them.

editorial.title: Bubble Sort Algorithm
editorial.intuition: Bubble sort is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the array, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: repeatedly swap adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal bubble sort implementation: only swap adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order after a pass through the entire array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Bubble sort is a correct sorting algorithm because it repeatedly reduces the number of inversions in the array.
commonMistakes: Not iterating over the array at least once to ensure it is sorted.
Not swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.
